title: androsace henryi subsp. henryi
intro: Androsace henryi subsp. henryi, also known as Tibetan rock jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the primrose family native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Androsace henryi subsp. henryi include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
